title: [CI:DOCS] troubleshooting: mention overlay option for --rootfs
body:
* Mention overlay option for --rootfs. Overlay description text
  is from commit 020d81f113ea1e11398ea77495cc4b8e05a91d38
  by Qi Wang

Signed-off-by: Erik Sjölund <erik.sjolund@gmail.com>

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]".  That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name.  Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

comments.body:
The troubleshooting tip currently has three

`podman run ...`

The first time
`--rm`
is used.

Should `--rm` be used in all `podman run ...` ?
@eriksjolund Should this be in troubleshooting ? I remember adding it in man pages here: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/11170

@flouthoc The text in the troubleshooting guide was already mentioning "overlay file system":
```
Another option would be to create an overlay file system on the directory as a lower and then
then allow podman to create the files on the upper.
```
I thought it would be better to replace that sentence with the something that mentioned `:O`

> @flouthoc The text in the troubleshooting guide was already mentioning "overlay file system":
> 
> ```
> Another option would be to create an overlay file system on the directory as a lower and then
> then allow podman to create the files on the upper.
> ```
> 
> I thought it would be better to replace that sentence with the something that mentioned `:O`

@eriksjolund Oh i see. Yes I think it would be good. Thanks for editing.
